title: Merck — RSV — briefing Q4-2025
html:
Enflonsia Briefing: Q4 2025 (Oct-Dec 2025)

Governing Thought

Enflonsia's first in-season quarter (Oct-Dec 2025) revealed the fundamental challenge: strong message resonance trapped inside inadequate reach. PET Wave 1 (Q4 '25) showed Enflonsia's dosing message landing well with HCPs who heard it, but Beyfortus's head start in rep deployment, NPP infrastructure, and practice relationships created a reach gap that messaging quality alone cannot close. Meanwhile, two external developments reshaped the landscape: Synagis exited the market (Dec 31, 2025) and CDC immunization schedule changes were announced.

Key Events This Quarter

Date Event Significance
Oct 2, 2025 Enflonsia available on VFC contract Opens government/public payer channel
Oct 2025 93% commercial insurance coverage achieved Removes major access barrier
Oct 2025 2025-26 RSV season begins Enflonsia's first live season
Q4 '25 PET Wave 1 fielded (Oct-Dec 2025) First promotional effectiveness data on Enflonsia vs. Beyfortus
Dec 2025 FDA notifies Merck, AZ, Sanofi of RSV safety investigation Regulatory uncertainty for both products
Dec 31, 2025 Synagis (palivizumab) discontinued High-risk infant coverage now exclusively Beyfortus (or Enflonsia where stocked)

PET Wave 1 Findings (Q4 '25)

Promotional Reach -- Beyfortus Dominated

  • Enflonsia PP reach: ~45-50% of MDs (est. from Q4 '25 baseline)
  • Beyfortus PP reach: ~75-80%
  • NPP reach gap even wider
  • Enflonsia's share of voice: minority position in a Beyfortus-heavy landscape

Message Recall -- Enflonsia's Identity Crystallized

  • ENF2 (same dose regardless of weight) emerged as the dominant message from the first wave
  • Storage and handling messages (ENF8, ENF9) also resonated
  • Efficacy message (ENF3) recalled but associated more with Beyfortus than Enflonsia by some HCPs

HCP Perceptions (Q4 '25 baseline)

  • Enflonsia advantages recognized: Dosing/admin, shelf-life, out-of-fridge time
  • Beyfortus advantages recognized: Efficacy, safety
  • Key gap: Rep performance -- Enflonsia reps trailed across most metrics in Wave 1

Stocking Barriers (Q4 '25)

  • #1 barrier: "Committed to/currently using Beyfortus" -- highest proportion
  • #2: Financial uncertainty
  • Most practices had already placed Beyfortus orders before Enflonsia was available
  • Mid-season switching was essentially zero

Beyfortus Competitive Position

  • Beyfortus reps delivered real-world evidence messages (BEYF12: 6M+ infants) to high effect
  • ACIP/AAP recommendation messaging (BEYF9) was the most frequently delivered
  • Beyfortus maintained strong brand association across efficacy, safety, and indication messages

Digital Landscape Evolution (Q4 '25 vs. Q3 '25)

What Changed

  • Enflonsia digital presence expanded from "brand new" to "established but small"
  • Email campaigns evolved from ordering/CDC contract to coverage information and clinical data
  • Banner ad messaging shifted from "NOW APPROVED" to "Choose ENFLONSIA for your appropriate patients"
  • Beyfortus maintained dominant digital presence across all channels

Paid Search Progress

  • Enflonsia maintained paid search strategy on Beyfortus and general RSV keywords
  • Beyfortus continued to dominate branded search terms
  • Both brands competing for limited pool of RSV-searching HCPs

External Developments

Synagis Discontinuation (Dec 31, 2025)

  • Palivizumab (Synagis) removed from market
  • High-risk infant population (~2-3% of births) now must use Beyfortus or Enflonsia
  • Beyfortus has second-season indication; Enflonsia does not -- this gives Beyfortus structural advantage for high-risk segment

FDA Safety Investigation (Dec 2025)

  • FDA notified Merck, AstraZeneca, and Sanofi of safety review into their RSV products
  • Clinical evidence has generally supported safety profiles of both products
  • Creates background uncertainty but no asymmetric impact (yet)
  • Worth monitoring for any differential findings

CDC Immunization Schedule Changes (Announced for Jan 2026 implementation)

  • RSV vaccine recommendations now based on "risk-benefit profiles"
  • Signals potential politicization of childhood immunization recommendations
  • HCP community beginning to fracture on CDC vs. AAP guidance

Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Sanofi/AZ Beyfortus (nirsevimab) ~$400M Strong Peak RSV season; established as standard of care for infant RSV
Pfizer Abrysvo (RSVpreF) ~$300M Moderate Primarily older adults; maternal uptake disappointing (~20-25%)
GSK Arexvy (RSV, older adults) ~$600M Strong Maintaining leadership in older adult RSV
Enflonsia First in-season quarter — Launch quarter; reach gap documented in PET Wave 1

Sanofi/AstraZeneca — Beyfortus (~$400M)

Sanofi Q4 2025 earnings commentary:

  1. Beyfortus established as standard of care in its first full RSV season. ~$400M in Q4 (peak season) reflects strong in-season demand. CDC's universal recommendation is driving broad adoption, and Sanofi reported high payer coverage across commercial and government channels.

  2. Supply constraints fully resolved. The reservation program and expanded manufacturing capacity ensured consistent availability throughout the 2025-26 season — eliminating the supply vulnerability that could have benefited Enflonsia.

  3. Beyfortus RWE base growing rapidly. Sanofi emphasized 6M+ infants immunized and 40+ real-world evidence studies. This RWE advantage is showing up in ZoomRx data: Beyfortus's BEYF10 message ("unparalleled body of RWE") surged +26% in message effectiveness in PET Wave 1.

Implication for Merck: Beyfortus's Q4 peak-season performance sets the benchmark Enflonsia must match. The $400M quarterly figure at peak season, combined with resolved supply and growing RWE, means Enflonsia's competitive path depends on winning pre-season stocking decisions for 2026-27 — not converting practices mid-season.

Pfizer — Abrysvo (~$300M)

Pfizer Q4 2025 earnings commentary:

  1. Older adult indication driving the majority of Abrysvo revenue. Of the ~$300M, the bulk came from the 60+ RSV vaccination market, not maternal use. Pfizer acknowledged that maternal uptake reached only 20-25% of eligible pregnancies.

  2. Maternal approach losing to monoclonal approach. Pfizer's own commentary confirmed that pediatricians and OB/GYNs prefer direct infant protection (Beyfortus/Enflonsia) over maternal vaccination. This is structural, not promotional — the clinical community has chosen.

  3. Pfizer facing three-way competition in older adults. Arexvy (GSK), Abrysvo (Pfizer), and mResvia (Moderna) are all competing for the 60+ RSV market. This fragmentation further pressures Abrysvo's commercial trajectory.

Implication for Merck: The maternal vs. monoclonal debate is effectively settled. This is good news for Enflonsia — the total addressable infant RSV market is Beyfortus + Enflonsia, with no meaningful third mechanism. Merck's challenge is market share within monoclonals, not market definition.

GSK — Arexvy (~$600M)

GSK's Arexvy had a strong peak season (~$600M in Q4), maintaining leadership in older adult RSV prevention. The CDC recommendation for the 60+ population and two-season durability data are strengthening Arexvy's position. Competition from Abrysvo and mResvia has not yet dented Arexvy's first-mover advantage in older adults.

Implication for Merck: Arexvy's success in older adults is irrelevant to Enflonsia's infant market. It does, however, demonstrate that RSV prevention products can sustain strong seasonal revenue once established — a positive long-term signal for Enflonsia's market potential.

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. Beyfortus at $400M peak-season is the revenue target for what a dominant infant RSV product can achieve. Enflonsia's long-term commercial potential is benchmarked against this figure.

  2. The maternal vaccination approach has lost. Abrysvo's 20-25% maternal uptake vs. Beyfortus's universal adoption confirms that the market has chosen monoclonal antibodies for infant RSV. Enflonsia entered the right mechanism class.

  3. Enflonsia's first in-season quarter was structurally disadvantaged — PET data shows Beyfortus's reach, RWE, and practice relationships were overwhelming advantages mid-season. The real competitive test is pre-season 2026-27, when stocking decisions reset.

So What for ZoomRx

  1. Q4 '25 is the "before" picture -- This is the baseline wave against which all Enflonsia progress will be measured. Q1 '26 data (next wave) will show first directional trends.
  2. Mid-season entry was structurally disadvantaged -- Most stocking decisions were made July-September. Enflonsia was too late for 2025-26 to be a fair test.
  3. The real test is pre-season 2026 -- Watch Q2-Q3 '26 stocking intent data. If "committed to Beyfortus" barrier continues declining (it dropped 26% Q4->Q1), Enflonsia has a real shot in 2026-27.
  4. Synagis exit opens high-risk segment -- But only for the product with second-season indication. Until Enflonsia files, this is Beyfortus territory.
  5. FDA investigation is a wildcard -- Track for any asymmetric findings between products.

Beyfortus at $400M peak-season is the revenue target for what a dominant infant RSV product can achieve. Enflonsia's long-term commercial potential is benchmarked against this figure.
